implementation_summary: Added addContactByNpub(npub, ownPubkeyHex): AddContactResult to app/src/lib/contacts.ts, plus the exported AddContactResult union type, matching stories.json seams[0] exactly. Algorithm: (1) decode via npubToPubkeyHex; validate result is well-formed 64-char lowercase hex (/^[0-9a-f]{64}$/) — reject as invalid_npub (no storage touch) on null OR malformed payload (e.g. checksummed-but-short npub); (2) case-insensitive self-check against ownPubkeyHex, no storage touch; (3) gather ALL case-insensitive key matches via filter; (4) if ANY match is active -> already_exists, no mutation; (5) all matches archived -> rememberKnownPeers([pubkeyHex]) THEN unarchiveContact THEN rememberContact on first archived match (reactivated: true); (6) no match -> rememberKnownPeers([pubkeyHex]) THEN rememberContact (reactivated: false). rememberKnownPeers-before-any-contacts-store-write ordering (ADR-005) preserved in both success branches. Two lead-owned review remediation rounds applied: (R1, Opus) case-insensitive resolution + JSDoc correction + honest test comment; (R2, Codex) 64-hex-char malformed-npub validation + all-active-match guard against case-variant duplicates.

lint_typecheck.typecheck: clean (npx tsc --noEmit, 0 errors, unchanged from baseline)

judgment_calls.decision: Existing-contact lookup gathers ALL case-insensitive key matches (filter), rejecting if any is active and reactivating only when all matches are archived.
judgment_calls.rationale: Consistent with the rest of contacts.ts, which compares pubkeys case-insensitively (commonGroups, eligibleGroupsForContact, listContacts) because stored keys are NOT normalized to lowercase at write time (rememberContact/rememberContactsFromGroups index by caller casing). Prevents duplicate-entry creation and duplicate-active records.
judgment_calls.residual_risk: On reactivation of a mixed/upper-case-keyed archived entry, the store retains the original mixed-case key while the function returns lowercase pubkeyHex. A downstream getContact() (exact-match on pubkeyHex) could miss it. Pre-existing module-wide case-normalization gap, low impact for this feature (S2 refreshes via listContacts which reads all entries regardless of key case, so the reactivated contact still appears). Disclosed as a Review note and flagged to S2; not fixed in S1 to avoid a module-wide change touching other callers.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The initial implementation shipped an exact-key contact lookup that looked reasonable but was wrong: contacts.ts stored keys are NOT lowercase-normalized at write time (rememberContact/rememberContactsFromGroups index by caller casing), yet the module compares case-insensitively everywhere else. This latent invariant was not documented at the call site, so it took two review rounds (Opus, then Codex) to surface — first the exact-key vs case-insensitive gap, then the all-active-match refinement and the malformed-npub-payload gap in npubToPubkeyHex.
retrospective.surprised_by: npubToPubkeyHex returns decoded data for ANY checksummed bech32 npub regardless of payload length (nip19.npubEncode("aa") decodes to "aa", not null), so callers must validate 64-hex before trusting the result.

absorbed_from.note: initial addContactByNpub implementation + 10 tests, correct on first pass for the happy paths
R1 Opus: exact-key lookup is a real duplicate-contact bug given un-normalized stored keys; JSDoc stated a false premise; test comment overstated ordering coverage

R2 Codex: malformed-payload npub bypasses invalid_npub (add 64-hex validation); .find could reactivate an archived variant when an active case-variant also exists (gather all matches, reject if any active)

review.stage1_opus: clean after R1+R2 (1 residual sev-3 out-of-scope, disclosed)

verification_summary.verdicts: 16 YES @1.0, 1 PARTIAL(VQ-S1-017 sev3 conf0.8 defense-in-depth, hardened by lead)
